{{Quote|The flame of a warrior burns bright in you, Tesar. You have shown that you will never falter or abandon a stricken comrade. May the Force guide you in all you do.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref>}}
'''Tesar Sebatyne''' was a [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who was [[Padawan/Legends|trained]] under his mother, [[Saba Sebatyne]], and flew in their squadron of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] users, the [[Wild Knights]].

==Biography==
===Yuuzhan Vong War===
Due to Barabel mating customs, Tesar shared a father with either [[Bela Hara]] or [[Krasov Hara]]. It was impossible to know which one Tesar could call his sister. Nevertheless, all three were hatchmates and had a sibling-like bond.

In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Duro (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Duro]], Tesar, his hatchmates and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Izal Waz]] assisted [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] in escaping [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and escorting their [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]] [[Eelysa]].

Two years into the war, Tesar, his hatchmates, and the [[Wild Knights]] entered the war effort in visible fashion. Tesar, Bela, and Krasov made an impression on their fellow Jedi with their impressive coordination and tactics through the [[Force-meld|battle meld]] during the [[Battle in the Froz system|skirmish]] at [[Froz]].

As the threat of the [[voxyn]] became clear to the Wild Knights in the wake of Master Eelysa's death, Tesar opted to go on the [[Myrkr strike team|strike team]] to [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]], and was one of the few who survived. Sadly, both his hatchmates died during the [[Mission to Myrkr|mission]].

After a brief stint on [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] following Myrkr and the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], Tesar rejoined the Wild Knights and fought bravely during the [[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; Second)|campaign]] at [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]] under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]].

As the war wore on into its third year, he was formally given the rank of Jedi Knight in a ceremony on [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and fought under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]]'s command as a flight leader in [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo's]] [[Twin Suns Squadron]]. At the [[Battle of Ebaq]] 9, Tesar was stranded alongside Jaina and [[Lowbacca]] inside the bunker on the surface of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. He was incapacitated during the fight against [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]].

Tesar alternated between Twin Suns Squadron and the Wild Knights for the remainder of the war, and piloted a [[Zonama Sekotan fighter]] at the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] during the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]].

===Dark Nest Crisis===
In [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], Tesar, Lowbacca, and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] arrested the [[Yaka]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Redstar]] on his ship, the ''[[Night Lady]]'', and attended his trial on the maximum security [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[MaxSec Eight]]. During the trial they received a mysterious [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-call from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Along with the other survivors of the Myrkr strike team, they set out to discover the source of the call, and found that it was the creature which had once been their fellow Jedi [[Raynar Thul]], believed slain at Myrkr, but now transformed into the [[Prime Unu]] of the hive-minded [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] [[Species/Legends|species]].

Along with [[Tekli]], [[Alema Rar]], Jaina Solo, [[Zekk]], Lowbacca, and [[Tahiri Veila]], Tesar was coerced into joining the Killiks by [[Raynar Thul|UnuThul's]] exploitation of the Myrkr battle meld, and he became a "[[Joiner]]"—someone who was influenced and was subject to the control of [[the Colony]], subjected to Force and pheromonal manipulation so profound as to physically affect brain-structure. The Joiners then helped the Killiks to defend their nests against the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]]. However, Tesar was convinced to return to known space by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. After the [[Qoribu Truce]], Tesar and Lowbacca went on to hunt pirates hijacking [[Abaarian]] water shipments.

Just as Jaina and Zekk came to form a [[Wikipedia:Dyad (sociology)|dyad]] within the Joiner meld, Tesar seemed to grow particularly close to Lowbacca and Tahiri. During the closing months of the Killik conflict, the three of them conspired to reveal Raynar's survival to [[Aryn Dro Thul]], his powerful mother. Tesar and Lowbacca were the ones to tell Madame Thul about the [[New Jedi Order]]'s possible plans to assassinate her son, because Raynar, as UnuThul, was believed to either intentionally or inadvertently be causing the [[Gorog|Dark Nest]] to keep coming back.

When Luke Skywalker found out what the three Jedi had done, he viewed their actions as a betrayal of their allegiance to the Jedi Order by undermining his attempts to bring unity to the Knights as [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]. Tesar and his friends attempted to redeem themselves by telling Luke about [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo's]] attack on [[Supply Depot Thrago]], but to no avail. Luke sent them to [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] to meditate on what it meant to be a Jedi. He told them that he would call them once he felt they were ready to rejoin the order, and that they were not to leave until they felt that call.

===Second Galactic Civil War===
By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Tesar and Lowbacca were operating under Admiral [[Nek Bwua'tu]]'s command. They were specifically tasked with monitoring the secret [[Kiris shipyards]]. Tesar was one of the many Jedi Knights present at the [[Funeral of Mara Jade Skywalker]], where his mother delivered the eulogy. Sebatyne later was part of a huge battle meld at the [[Second Battle of Balmorra]].

Towards the end of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Tesar sparred with fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Lowbacca and [[Zekk]] against Jaina Solo, in preparation for her coming battle with [[Darth Caedus]], who Tesar had known as Jacen Solo before his fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Tesar was also involved while Jaina learned to control the power of [[Shatterpoint/Legends|shatterpoints]]. The war concluded following the death of Caedus, whom Jaina successfully bested in battle.

===Picking up the pieces (43-44 ABY)===
In 43 ABY, Tesar was one of the Barabels along side [[Wilyem]], [[Zal]], and [[Dordi]] that helped to get the mad Jedi off [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and to the Jedi facility on [[Shedu Maad]], when [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Natasi Daala]] wanted to freeze all of them in [[Carbonite/Legends|Carbonite]]. They later made a nest inside the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]].

===The fight for Coruscant===
When [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] directed the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to leave [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] as a trap for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], he ordered the four [[Barabel/Legends|Barabels]] (Tesar, Wilyem, Zal, and Dordi) to hunt and kill every [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] that came across their path.

When [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana Solo]] saw a vision of Sith attacking and killing all of the [["Hatchling" maintenance droid|Hatchlings]], she and her grandparents Han and Leia Solo returned to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to inform [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bazel Warv]] so he could go and tell the Barabels of the impending attack, but they were ambushed at the secret exit of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] which was lead by [[Vestara Khai]]. Jedi Warv died defending the [[Solo family/Legends|House of Solo]] from the Sith, dying a hero for [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana Solo]].

When the [[Parting Gift]] came to rescue the group along with a lot of Hatchlings, Tesar Sebatyne told him that his baby Barabels liked [[Jagged Fel]].

===Leaving Coruscant===
Tesar was at the [[Wedding of Jagged Fel and Jaina Solo]] and most likely was one of the Jedi who lit his Lightsaber for [[Jaina Solo Fel]] to walk under.

He along with his mother [[Saba Sebatyne]], and the [["Hatchling" maintenance droid|Hatchlings]] and his nest mates [[Wilyem]], [[Zal]], [[Dordi]] left [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and went to [[Shedu Maad]].
